steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says Thomas received the fellowship ‘because of his lifetime contributions to the arts.’ The blank describes what happened to Thomas — he was picked to receive the honor based on merit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — confused: means disoriented or baffled. Someone receiving an honor due to merit is not described as confused. ✗
  • Choice B — reluctant: means unwilling. The passage gives no hint Thomas was unwilling; the signal is that he earned the fellowship. ✗
  • Choice C — ineligible: means not qualified. This directly contradicts the passage — Thomas received the fellowship, so he was clearly eligible. ✗
  • Choice D — chosen: means selected. Substituting: Thomas was chosen for the fellowship because of his lifetime contributions. That matches the signal perfectly. ✓
That matches Choice D — chosen.
